message:
🐛 Fix kubeconfig watcher: add polling fallback and inode re-watch (#911)

The fsnotify-based kubeconfig watcher could silently fail on macOS when
tools like the OCI CLI perform atomic file replacements (write temp +
rename). After an atomic replace, the kqueue-based inode watch becomes
stale and no further events are delivered.

This fix adds three improvements:
1. Polling fallback (5s interval) that checks file mtime to catch any
   changes fsnotify misses — ensures detection within 5s worst case
2. Re-add the file to the watcher after each reload to re-establish
   the inode-level watch on the new file
3. Extract reloadAndNotify() for shared reload logic between fsnotify
   events and poll-based detection

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
